Title: Mechanism of sphingolipid homeostasis revealed by structural analysis of Arabidopsis SPT-ORM1 complex

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cryo-EM structural analysis; site-directed mutagenesis; in vitro SPT enzyme assays; ceramide analog inhibition assays; HEK293 SPTSSA knockout cell coexpression with d2-serine labeling and LC-MS/MS
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: negative regulation of sphingolipid biosynthesis through SPT repression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ORM1 physically associates with the Arabidopsis SPT complex and lowers its activity to about 50% of SPT alone. 2. Ceramide binds at the ORM1-LCB2a interface and is critical for strong ORM1-dependent repression of SPT. 3. The ORM1 N terminus stabilizes an inhibitory conformation that blocks acyl-CoA entry, linking ceramide sensing to sphingolipid homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ORM1 is not a transcription factor or catalytic pathway enzyme; it acts as a ceramide-sensing inhibitory subunit. C6-phytoceramide inhibits more strongly than C6-ceramide, whereas C6-dihydroceramide shows little effect. HEK293 cells were used as a proxy system for cellular validation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced SPT activity could limit sphingolipid production needed for growth if overrepressed; insufficient repression may permit buildup of PCD-inducing intermediates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sphingolipids are required for growth and basal cellular functions; ceramide/LCB homeostasis mitigates accumulation of PCD-inducing intermediates and is linked to hypersensitive response pathogen resistance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ORM1 and its ceramide-sensing residues may be engineered to tune sphingolipid homeostasis and balance growth versus defense-associated sphingolipid accumulation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS lipid analysis; no public accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as an essential subunit of serine palmitoyltransferase, which condenses L-serine and palmitoyl-CoA in the first committed and rate-limiting step of sphingolipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies the initial long-chain-base precursor pool for all downstream sphingolipids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: part of the rate-limiting entry enzyme complex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant complex purification; in vitro enzyme assay; cryo-EM structure of AtSPT-ORM1 complex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: participates in the rate-limiting SPT complex that controls flux into sphingolipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LCB1 is a core structural and catalytic subunit of Arabidopsis SPT. 2. It forms the AtSPT complex with LCB2a and SPTssa and the AtSPT-ORM1 complex with added ORM1. 3. Through the SPT complex it contributes to control of sphingolipid production.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Alpha-oxoamine synthase family SPT large subunit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as part of the complex rather than as an individually perturbed gene
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: myriocin inhibition; ceramide analog treatments on complex
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong reduction in SPT function would be expected to impair essential sphingolipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating the SPT complex may alter sphingolipid flux, but likely requires caution because this is the essential rate-limiting entry step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sphingolipids support growth, basal cellular functions, and defense-related homeostasis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the catalytic PLP-containing subunit of serine palmitoyltransferase that condenses L-serine and palmitoyl-CoA to initiate sphingolipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly generates the initial precursor for long-chain bases and downstream ceramides/sphingolipids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cryo-EM structure; enzyme kinetics; N-terminal deletion mutagenesis in complex; ceramide analog inhibition assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LCB2a is the catalytic SPT subunit whose interaction with ORM1 and ceramide modulates sphingolipid biosynthetic flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LCB2a forms the catalytic core of Arabidopsis SPT and carries the PLP cofactor. 2. Ceramide binds at the interface between LCB2a and ORM1. 3. The LCB2a N terminus forms a hybrid beta sheet with the ORM1 N terminus that helps stabilize ORM1-mediated inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Alpha-oxoamine synthase family PLP-dependent SPT large subunit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lys311 in LCB2a binds PLP according to the structure; LCB2a-ΔN5 weakens ORM1-dependent repression by disrupting the hybrid beta sheet
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing catalytic function would impair essential sphingolipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A mechanistic target for tuning sphingolipid entry flux, though likely with pleiotropic risk because it is essential and rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sphingolipids are essential membrane lipids and signaling-related metabolites in growth and defense

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Accessory small subunit of the serine palmitoyltransferase complex required for full SPT complex assembly and activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for functional SPT complex that produces sphingolipid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: part of the rate-limiting SPT complex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant complex reconstitution; cryo-EM structure; HEK293 SPTSSA knockout complementation context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SPTssa supports formation of active Arabidopsis SPT and AtSPT-ORM1 complexes enabling sphingolipid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPTssa is a constitutive component of the Arabidopsis SPT and SPT-ORM1 complexes. 2. Its presence is required to reconstitute active complexes for biochemical and structural analysis. 3. The HEK293 SPTSSA knockout system was used to test Arabidopsis SPT regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not dissect an independent metabolic effect of SPTssa mutations in Arabidopsis; it is included because it is a core experimentally reconstituted subunit of the focal complex
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation may broadly alter essential sphingolipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for engineering SPT complex activity, but direct utility is less defined than ORM1-mediated control
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sphingolipids are essential for membrane function and homeostasis
